content: read reviews/241.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/241.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/241.md.

    30→- [x] `erdos241F` has a LaTeX docstring defining f(N) as the maximum cardinality.

    41→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_241` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    42→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
    43→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring referencing source papers ([Er61, Er69, …], [BoCh62], [Gr01]).
    44→
    45→### `answer()` usage
    46→
    47→The source problem asks: *"Is it true that f(N) ~ N^{1/3}?"* — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    48→
    49→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (Case 1 correctly applied).
    50→- [x] RHS formalizes the question as asked: f(N) ~ N^{1/3} expressed as an ε-characterization: for all ε > 0, eventually (1-ε)N^{1/3} ≤ f(N) ≤ (1+ε)N^{1/3}. This is equivalent to f(N)/N^{1/3} → 1.
    51→- [x] `answer(sorry)` is unsolved (problem is still open, confirmed by source).
    52→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ ε`, `∀ᶠ N`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS, not as theorem parameters.

    59→
    60→## 8. AMS Attribute
    61→
    62→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 11`.
    63→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 11).
    64→- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5 < 11.
    65→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    66→- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (5 = combinatorics, 11 = number theory). Appropriate for a B₃ set / additive combinatorics problem.
    67→
    68→## 9. Proof Bodies
    69→
    70→- [x] Uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (acceptable per checklist).
    71→- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
    72→- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
    73→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    74→

    76→
    77→- N/A. No variants present. The problem is a single question and does not require variants.
    78→
    79→## 11. Code Quality
    80→
    81→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    82→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∈`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `≤`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ε`, `⊆`.
    83→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    84→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    85→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    86→- [x] File ends with content on the last line (single trailing newline expected).
    87→

    89→
    90→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
    91→- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.
    92→
    93→## Mathematical Accuracy
    94→
    95→The formalization is faithful to the source problem:
    96→
    97→- **B₃ set definition** (`IsB3Set`): Correctly captures that all 3-element sums from A are distinct up to reordering, by requiring sorted triples (a₁ ≤ a₂ ≤ a₃) and (b₁ ≤ b₂ ≤ b₃) with equal sums to be element-wise identical. Allows repeated elements (non-strict inequality), matching standard B_h set conventions.
    98→- **f(N) definition** (`erdos241F`): Uses `sSup` over cardinalities of B₃ subsets of `Finset.Icc 1 N`. The set is nonempty (contains 0 from ∅) and bounded above (by N), so `sSup` on `ℕ` yields the correct maximum.
    99→- **Asymptotic statement**: f(N) ~ N^{1/3} is correctly formalized via the ε-characterization with `∀ᶠ N in atTop`, equivalent to f(N)/N^{1/3} → 1.
   100→- **Problem status**: Correctly marked `research open`. The problem remains open — the known bounds are (1+o(1))N^{1/3} ≤ f(N) ≤ ((7/2)^{1/3}+o(1))N^{1/3}, leaving a gap. Confirmed open as of the source page.
   101→
   102→## Verdict
   103→
   104→**PASS.** All checkable items pass. The formalization is clean, mathematically accurate, and fully conforms to the style guidelines. Build verification deferred.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/241.lean — Erdős Problem 241

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 241`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/241`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (B₃ sets, maximum size of A ⊆ {1,…,N} with distinct triple sums).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: [Er61, Er69, Er70b, Er70c, Er73, Er77c, ErGr80], [BoCh62], [Gr01] with full citation details.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos241 ... end Erdos241`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos241`).
- [x] `open Filter` appears **before** the namespace block (line 45).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsB3Set` has a LaTeX docstring explaining the B₃ property via sorted triples.
- [x] `erdos241F` has a LaTeX docstring defining f(N) as the maximum cardinality.
- [x] `erdos241F` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sSup`, which involves classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `IsB3Set` uses `UpperCamelCase` (returns `Prop`).
- [x] `erdos241F` uses `lowerCamelCase` (returns `ℕ`).
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; N/A for `local notation`.
- [x] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks, but these are optional.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_241` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring referencing source papers ([Er61, Er69, …], [BoCh62], [Gr01]).

### `answer()` usage

The source problem asks: *"Is it true that f(N) ~ N^{1/3}?"* — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P` (Case 1 correctly applied).
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as asked: f(N) ~ N^{1/3} expressed as an ε-characterization: for all ε > 0, eventually (1-ε)N^{1/3} ≤ f(N) ≤ (1+ε)N^{1/3}. This is equivalent to f(N)/N^{1/3} → 1.
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is unsolved (problem is still open, confirmed by source).
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ ε`, `∀ᶠ N`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` — valid category for an unsolved problem.
- [x] Theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 11).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5 < 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (5 = combinatorics, 11 = number theory). Appropriate for a B₃ set / additive combinatorics problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A. No variants present. The problem is a single question and does not require variants.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∈`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `≤`, `∧`, `↔`, `→`, `ε`, `⊆`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with content on the last line (single trailing newline expected).

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

## Mathematical Accuracy

The formalization is faithful to the source problem:

- **B₃ set definition** (`IsB3Set`): Correctly captures that all 3-element sums from A are distinct up to reordering, by requiring sorted triples (a₁ ≤ a₂ ≤ a₃) and (b₁ ≤ b₂ ≤ b₃) with equal sums to be element-wise identical. Allows repeated elements (non-strict inequality), matching standard B_h set conventions.
- **f(N) definition** (`erdos241F`): Uses `sSup` over cardinalities of B₃ subsets of `Finset.Icc 1 N`. The set is nonempty (contains 0 from ∅) and bounded above (by N), so `sSup` on `ℕ` yields the correct maximum.
- **Asymptotic statement**: f(N) ~ N^{1/3} is correctly formalized via the ε-characterization with `∀ᶠ N in atTop`, equivalent to f(N)/N^{1/3} → 1.
- **Problem status**: Correctly marked `research open`. The problem remains open — the known bounds are (1+o(1))N^{1/3} ≤ f(N) ≤ ((7/2)^{1/3}+o(1))N^{1/3}, leaving a gap. Confirmed open as of the source page.

## Verdict

**PASS.** All checkable items pass. The formalization is clean, mathematically accurate, and fully conforms to the style guidelines. Build verification deferred.

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
